transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Three problems: (1) VE: compute P(B7=1|evidence) via variable elimination - need careful factor manipulation. (2) Steiner: find minimum cost tree connecting Port, Mill, Cape with optimal frequency assignment. (3) GraphCol: find 4-coloring with 0 conflicts (graph is 4-colorable). Baseline scores: VE needs exact answer, Steiner=57, GraphCol=18. Strategy: solve GraphCol first (0 conflicts = score 4, big gain), then Steiner (try to beat 57), then VE (exact computation). For Steiner: baseline uses Port-Rock(4)+Mill-Rock(2)+Cape-Port(6)=12 edge cost + 3 freqs*15=45, total=57. Can we do better? Try Mill-Port(7)+Cape-Port(6)=13 edges, 3 terminals only, 3 freqs needed due to Mill/Port and Cape/Port interference - need 3 colors minimum, so 13+45=58 worse. Baseline seems good. For GraphCol: attempt proper 4-coloring with 0 conflicts.

NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Solve all three: VE exact computation, Steiner tree optimization, GraphCol 4-coloring with 0 conflicts", "p_solve": 0.75, "time_budget_s": 580}
